title: Notify-on-alert — raise a notification event when a security incident opens (WF-015-NEG-006)
business_reason: A critical security incident (audit tamper / permission drift / suspicious login) must reach the security team, not just sit in a table. Raise a notification event on incident open via the existing fan-out.

design.helper: server/security/incidentNotify.ts notifyIncidentOpened(supabase, {id,code,summary,severity}, actor) -> raiseNotificationEvent(event_code 'security_incident_opened', idempotency_key per incident, source_entity security_incidents, recipient_resolver 'security_team').
design.wiring: called in all three runners (auditIntegrity, permissionDriftRun, suspiciousLoginsRun) ONLY when a NEW incident is inserted (not on reuse). Idempotency_key = incident code -> exactly one notification per incident; suppressed cleanly if no 'security_team' template is configured.

param_14.question: Does a critical security incident actually reach someone?
param_14.answer: Yes. Opening a security incident raises a 'security_incident_opened' notification event (idempotent per incident) that fans out to the security team via the existing notification pipeline. If no security-team template is configured yet the event is raised and suppressed cleanly (no delivery, but recorded + auditable).
param_14.answer_source: incidentNotify.ts, raiseNotificationEvent, the three security runners
param_14.security_implications: incidents are actively surfaced; one notification per incident (no storm); raised on NEW incidents only.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: an approved 'security_incident_opened' template + a security-team recipient resolver to enable actual delivery; escalation on unacknowledged.

verify: npx playwright test notify_on_alert permission_drift suspicious_login
